message.content:
Adversarially VERIFY this research claim about how a top deep-research system scores on DeepResearch Bench.

CLAIM: Onyx is an open-source DRB submission (54.54 on the Gemini tab) that publishes its actual generated reports — a directly imitable structure corpus.
DETAIL: github.com/onyx-dot-app/onyx_deep_research_bench: reports live in report_logs/. Their stated setup: GPT-5.2 backbone, Serper for search, Firecrawl for crawling, 30-minute cap per question, max 2 minutes between user-facing outputs, and — notably — 'Outputs were tuned for human usability and conciseness - the reports are typically around 10,000 tokens'. The repo README claims 54.92/rank-1 (self-reported); the current Gemini CSV lists onyx at 54.54 (TrajectoryKit holds 54.92). Worth pulling report_logs to measure their paragraph/H3/table/bullet distribution against the human reference profile we already computed.
SYSTEM: other
SOURCE GIVEN: https://github.com/onyx-dot-app/onyx_deep_research_bench
STATED CONFIDENCE: medium

Go to the source. Check it actually says this. Check it is CURRENT (the benchmark's leaderboard and evaluator both
changed in 2026 — the original paper's numbers are stale; Gemini-2.5-Pro was replaced by GPT-5.5 as the RACE judge).
Default to UNSUPPORTED if you cannot confirm it from a primary source. We would rather have five verified facts
than thirty plausible ones.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt: Quote the README verbatim: what score/rank does it claim on DeepResearch Bench? What model backbone, search/crawl providers, time caps, and token-length tuning are stated? Is there a report_logs/ directory?

content.content:
# Onyx Deep Research Benchmark Claims

**Rank and Score:**
Onyx claims rank 1 with an overall score of 54.92, stating "Onyx" achieved the highest marks across comprehensiveness, insight, and instruction following.

**Technical Details:**
The repository specifies: "LLM: GPT-5.2" and "Web Search API: Serper" with "Web Crawler: Firecrawl" as external dependencies.

**Operational Constraints:**
Per the documentation: "All research + answer generations for any given question are capped to 30 minutes maximum" and "outputs were tuned for human usability and conciseness - the reports are typically around 10,000 tokens (though the longest can be upwards of 20,000 tokens)."

**Directory Confirmation:**
Yes, the README explicitly mentions: "The `report_logs` directory contains all of the reports along with all of the research agent tasks."

content.content:
Onyx Deep Research Benchmark Results

# Overview This repository contains the [Onyx](https://www.onyx.app/) submission to the [Deep Research Benchmark](https://github.com/Ayanami0730/deep_research_bench). The latest leaderboard can be accessed here: [Deep Research Benchmark Leaderboard](https://huggingface.co/spaces/muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard). # Benchmark Performance | Rank | Model | Overall | Comprehensiveness | Insight | Instruction Following | Readability | |:----:|-------|:-------:|:----:|:-------:|:--:|:----:| | 1 | Onyx | **54.92** 🥇 | **55.07** 🥇 | **57.10** 🥇 | **52.99** 🥇 | *52.41* 🥈 | | 2 | Cellcog | *54.54* 🥈 | *54.43* 🥈 | *56.22* 🥈 | *52.76* 🥈 | **53.14** 🥇 | | 3 | Qianfan-DeepResearch Pro| 54.22 | **55.07** 🥇 | 56.09 | 51.77 | 52.12 | | 4 | Qianfan-DeepResearch | 53.07 | 52.65 | 55.44 | 51.61 | 51.21 | | 5 | Tavily Research | 52.44 | 52.84 | 53.59 | 51.92 | 49.21 | | 6 | Thinkdepthai DeepResearch | 52.43 | 52.02 | 53.88 | 52.04 | 50.12 | | 7 | Salesforce AIR | 50.65 | 50.00 | 51.09 | 50.77 | 50.32 | | 8 | LangChain Open Deep Research (GPT-5, with Gensee search) | 50.60 | 50.06 | 50.76 | 51.31 | 49.72 | | 9 | Gemini 2.5 Pro Deep Research | 49.71 | 49.51 | 49.45 | 50.12 | 50.00 | | 10 | LangChain Open Deep Research (GPT-5 with Tavily) | 49.33 | 49.80 | 47.34 | 51.05 | 48.99 | > **Legend:** 🥇 = best in column · 🥈 = second best ### Other Notable Mentions: | Rank | Model | Overall | Comprehensiveness | Insight | Instruction Following | Readability | |:----:|-------|:-------:|:----:|:-------:|:--:|:----:| | 11 | OpenAI Deep Research | 46.45 | 46.46 | 43.73 | 49.39 | 47.22 | | 12 | Claude Research | 45.00 | 45.34 | 42.79 | 47.58 | 44.66 | | 17 | Perplexity Deep Research | 40.46 | 39.1 | 35.65 | 46.11 | 43.08 | # Onyx Deep Research

# Additional Info Onyx is a production system with an emphasis on user experience. Additional product/UX constraints that we feel are important which were taken into account for the generation of the answers for the benchmark are as follows: - All research + answer generations for any given question are capped to 30 minutes maximum. - Outputs were tuned for human usability and conciseness - the reports are typically around 10,000 tokens (though the longest can be upwards of 20,000 tokens). - The biggest allowed latency between any user facing output is 2 minutes. ## Reproducing Results The reports submitted for the benchmark were produced using [this](https://github.com/onyx-dot-app/onyx/releases/tag/nightly-latest-20260207) nightly build of Onyx. External dependencies used for this particular set of results were: - LLM: [GPT-5.2](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5/) - Web Search API: [Serper](https://serper.dev/) - Web Crawler: [Firecrawl](http://firecrawl.dev/) The same Deep Research flow can be run with other LLMs and Web Search/Crawler APIs (or Onyx's built in), but results will vary based on the setup. ## Files Included All of the standard files for the benchmark in their standard format are included under `eval_results`: - `onyx_raw_results.jsonl` contains the raw final outputs from the Onyx system. - `onyx_cleaned_results.jsonl` contains the same contents as above but citations are stripped. - `raw_results.jsonl` contains the scores across all of the metrics for each of the reports individually. - `race_result.txt` contains the final aggregate scores. The `report_logs` directory contains all of the reports along with all of the research agent tasks. The research agent tasks are unique to the Onyx system, they are not required for the benchmark and simply included for the reader's interest. # Acknowledgements Thank you to the DeepResearch Bench team for creating the benchmark and maintaining the leaderboard! > Du, M., Xu, B., Zhu, C., Wang, X., & Mao, Z. (2025). DeepResearch Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Deep Research Agents. *arXiv preprint* [arXiv:2506.11763](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.11763). **BibTeX:** ```bibtex @article{du2025deepresearch, title={DeepResearch Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Deep Research Agents}, author={Du, Mingxuan and Xu, Benfeng and Zhu, Chiwei and Wang, Xiaorui and Mao, Zhendong}, journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2506.11763}, year={2025}, url={https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.11763} } ``` === reports count/lines Query ID: 1 ============================================================ QUESTION: 收集整理目前中国9阶层实际收入和财务状况，特别研究得出中国的中产有哪些特点，实际中产人数，财力等等 ============================================================ RESEARCH TASKS (6 total): #1: 研究中国语境下“九阶层/九大阶层”的常见划分口径与可操作定义（如陆学艺十阶层/九阶层改写、按职业与权力/资本、按收入分位或家庭可支配收入倍数等），并总结每一层可量化的收入、资产、负债、社保、住房等判定指标；请优先使用权威学术/政策研究与主流智库资料，给出引用来源与年份。 #2: 收集2023-2026年关于中国居民收入分布、财富分布、资产负债结构的权威最新数据与报告（国家统计局住户调查与《中国统计年鉴》、人民银行金融统计/住户部门杠杆率、CHFS/CFPS/CHIP数据最新论文与报告、瑞信/瑞银或国内券商/研究机构财富报告等）；整理关键指标的口径差异（个人/家庭、税前/税后、城镇/农村、名义/实际）。 #3: 研究“中国中产”界定方法与规模测算：梳理国内外常见中产标准（收入分位50-150%中位数、PPP美元日收入、消费、职业教育、净资产等），并汇总近年（约2021-2026）对中国中产人数/家庭数估算的主要结论、区间与不确定性；特别关注可支配收入、净资产、可投资资产与债务负担等‘财力’指标。 #4: 补齐“居民资产负债/财富分布/杠杆率”在2023-2026可引用的数据：重点检索人民银行（含资金流量表或住户部门资产负债表）、国家资产负债表研究（中科院/社科院）、以及CHFS（中国家庭金融调查）公开报告/论文中关于家庭总资产、金融资产、住房资产、负债、净资产的分布（中位数、分位点、Top份额）与近年趋势；同时找出能引用的住户部门杠杆率（住户部门债务/GDP）权威来源与2023-2025最新数值。 #5: 核查并收集中国官方或权威统计机构对“中等收入群体/中产”的正式定义（如家庭年收入区间、可支配收入倍数、或其他口径）以及“4亿中等收入群体”说法的原始来源（国务院/发改委/统计局/领导人讲话等）；同时整理各来源的年份、口径与是否可复现。 #6: 寻找可操作的“九阶层”量化方案：检索近年（约2018-2026）学术论文/智库报告中将中国人群按收入/财富/职业划分为9档或9类的实证方法（例如按收入九分位、按家庭等价可支配收入的九分组、或将陆学艺十阶层合并为九阶层的规则），并提取每档对应的收入、资产、负债、消费、住房、社保等典型数值或区间。 ============================================================ ANSWER: 下面这份整理会尽量把**“九阶层/九大阶层”**、**收入与资产负债的“硬数据”**、以及**“中产”规模与画像**放到同一个框架里说明。但必须先把关键限制讲清楚：**中国并不存在一个全国统一、官方发布且可复现的“九阶层”名单与逐层收入/资产阈值表**。学界最常用的是陆学艺团队的“十大阶层”（职业—资源占有框架）[[1]](http://sociology.cssn.cn/xscg/zxwz/201011/t20101124_1986373.shtml)[[2]](https://html.rhhz.net/society/html/20180607.htm)；官方统计最可复用的是住户调查口径的**收入均值/中位数、五等份分组**等[[5]](https://www.stats.gov.cn/sj/zxfb/202601/t20260119_1962321.html)[[55]](https://www.stats.gov.cn/sj/zxfb/202302/t20230203_1899428.html)；财富/资产负债方面，宏观层面可用人民银行**金融账户（资金存量/流量表）**[[36]](https://www.pbc.gov.cn/diaochatongjisi/attachDir/2025/12/2025123116364867732.xlsx)[[39]](https://www.pbc.gov.cn/diaochatongjisi/attachDir/2025/11/2025110415393395158.xlsx)与NIFD宏观杠杆率报告[[46]](http://www.nifd.cn/Uploads/SeriesReport/e39bb5f0-0074-4e76-8eed-b0fe068d19c8.pdf)，微观分布则往往依赖CHFS/CFPS/CHIP等数据（但你这次给到的已采集材料里，**缺少一个“九档分组 + 各档资产负债统计”的现成权威表**）。 因此我会采用“**两步法**”回答你的问题： 1) 先把**权威可引用的最新收入、消费、金融资产负债、杠杆**数据底座搭好； 2) 在此基础上给出一套**可操作的“九阶层（九档）财务画像框架”**（明确哪些是权威数据、哪些是研究构造/推算口径），并重点把**中产**的特征、人数区间、财力边界讲清楚。 --- ## 1) 先对齐口径：你要的“阶层—收入—财务状况”到底有哪些常用定义？ ### 1.1 职业/权力—资源占有口径：陆学艺“十大阶层”（最常用的社会分层框架） 陆学艺团队用“职业分类”为基础，以对**组织资源、经济资源、文化资源**的占有状况为原则，划分十个社会阶层[[1]](http://sociology.cssn.cn/xscg/zxwz/201011/t20101124_1986373.shtml)： 1. 国家与社会管理者 2. 经理人员 3. 私营企业主 4. 专业技术人员 5. 办事人员 6. 个体工商户 7. 商业服务业员工 8. 产业工人 9. 农业劳动者

input.parameters:
{"verdict":"CONFIRMED","reasoning":"Every checkable element of the claim was verified against primary sources.

1) REPO EXISTS, IS OPEN SOURCE, PUBLISHES REPORTS. GitHub API on onyx-dot-app/onyx_deep_research_bench: MIT license, description "Onyx Submission for Deep Research Benchmark", created 2026-02-08, last push 2026-04-04, 24 stars. Top-level contents: LICENSE, README.md, assets/, eval_results/, report_logs/. `report_logs/` contains exactly 100 files (1.md ... 100.md, ~43-66 KB each) — one per DRB query. So "publishes its actual generated reports" is true and the corpus is complete (100/100).

2) README SETUP DETAILS — ALL VERBATIM-CONFIRMED from raw README (raw.githubusercontent.com/.../main/README.md):
 - "LLM: GPT-5.2", "Web Search API: Serper", "Web Crawler: Firecrawl"
 - "All research + answer generations for any given question are capped to 30 minutes maximum."
 - "Outputs were tuned for human usability and conciseness - the reports are typically around 10,000 tokens (though the longest can be upwards of 20,000 tokens)."
 - "The biggest allowed latency between any user facing output is 2 minutes." (the claim's "max 2 minutes between user-facing outputs")
 - "The `report_logs` directory contains all of the reports along with all of the research agent tasks."
 - Reproduced with Onyx nightly build nightly-latest-20260207.

3) SCORES. README self-reports Rank 1 / 54.92 overall (55.07 comp, 57.10 insight, 52.99 IF, 52.41 read) with Cellcog 2nd at 54.54 — that is a submission-time snapshot. Our current Gemini-2.5-Pro-judge CSV (drb_corpus/leaderboard_gemini25pro_judge.csv) lists: line 18 TrajectoryKit 54.92, line 19 onyx 54.54 (54.67 / 56.43 / 53.08 / 52.02). Exactly as the claim states — the README's rank-1/54.92 is stale self-report; onyx now sits at 54.54 on the Gemini tab, ~19th.

4) CURRENCY CHECK (the important caveat). Onyx does NOT appear at all in drb_corpus/leaderboard_gpt55_judge.csv — the current GPT-5.5 RACE-judge leaderboard. So onyx's 54.54 has standing only on the legacy Gemini-2.5-Pro judge tab; it has no score under the current evaluator, and the 54.92/rank-1 README number is doubly stale (old judge AND pre-dated snapshot). The claim was careful to say "54.54 on the Gemini tab," so it is not overstating — but any downstream use must not present onyx as a current-leaderboard rank.

5) USABILITY FOR STRUCTURE MINING (practical notes, not defects in the claim). report_logs/*.md are NOT pure reports: each file has a "Query ID / QUESTION / RESEARCH TASKS (N total) / ANSWER" header before the report body, so a parser must split on the ANSWER delimiter or the H3/bullet counts will be contaminated by the task list. A cleaner source for structural measurement is eval_results/onyx_cleaned_results.jsonl (citation-stripped articles) — and we already have the article set locally at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/onyx.jsonl (100 lines, {id, prompt, article}). Also note many reports are in Chinese (DRB prompts are bilingual), so token-length and paragraph statistics must be language-normalized before comparing to a human reference profile.","corrected":"Claim stands as written. Two precisions to carry forward: (a) the README's "54.92 / rank 1" is a submission-time snapshot under the OLD Gemini-2.5-Pro RACE judge — on the current Gemini CSV onyx is 54.54 (TrajectoryKit 54.92), and onyx does not appear on the current GPT-5.5-judge leaderboard at all, so it has no standing under the live evaluator; (b) report_logs/*.md files prepend a QUESTION + RESEARCH TASKS block before the ANSWER, so use eval_results/onyx_cleaned_results.jsonl (or the local /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/onyx.jsonl) for structural stats. Sources: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onyx-dot-app/onyx_deep_research_bench/main/README.md ; GitHub API repo + contents listings ; /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/leaderboard_gemini25pro_judge.csv and leaderboard_gpt55_judge.csv."}

input.reasoning:
Every checkable element verified against primary sources.

1) REPO: GitHub API on onyx-dot-app/onyx_deep_research_bench — MIT license, description "Onyx Submission for Deep Research Benchmark", created 2026-02-08, last push 2026-04-04, 24 stars. Top-level: LICENSE, README.md, assets/, eval_results/, report_logs/. report_logs/ contains exactly 100 files (1.md ... 100.md, ~43-66 KB each), one per DRB query. "Open-source DRB submission that publishes its actual generated reports" = TRUE, and the corpus is complete (100/100).

2) README SETUP — all verbatim-confirmed from raw.githubusercontent.com/onyx-dot-app/onyx_deep_research_bench/main/README.md: "LLM: GPT-5.2"; "Web Search API: Serper"; "Web Crawler: Firecrawl"; "All research + answer generations for any given question are capped to 30 minutes maximum."; "Outputs were tuned for human usability and conciseness - the reports are typically around 10,000 tokens (though the longest can be upwards of 20,000 tokens)."; "The biggest allowed latency between any user facing output is 2 minutes." (= the claim's max-2-min between user-facing outputs); "The `report_logs` directory contains all of the reports along with all of the research agent tasks." Built with Onyx nightly-latest-20260207.

3) SCORES: README self-reports Rank 1 / 54.92 (55.07 comp, 57.10 insight, 52.99 IF, 52.41 read), with Cellcog 2nd at 54.54 — a submission-time snapshot. Our current Gemini-judge CSV (/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/leaderboard_gemini25pro_judge.csv) has TrajectoryKit 54.92 (line 18) and onyx 54.54 (line 19: 54.67/56.43/53.08/52.02). Exactly as the claim states.

4) CURRENCY (key caveat): onyx does NOT appear anywhere in /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/leaderboard_gpt55_judge.csv — the current GPT-5.5 RACE-judge board. Its 54.54 exists only on the legacy Gemini-2.5-Pro tab; the README's rank-1/54.92 is doubly stale (old judge + pre-dated snapshot). The claim explicitly says "on the Gemini tab," so it does not overstate — but onyx must not be presented as holding a current-leaderboard rank.

5) PRACTICAL NOTES for the intended structure-mining use (not defects in the claim): report_logs/*.md are not pure reports — each prepends "Query ID / QUESTION / RESEARCH TASKS (N total)" before "ANSWER:", so paragraph/H3/bullet counts will be contaminated unless you split on the ANSWER delimiter. Cleaner: eval_results/onyx_cleaned_results.jsonl (citation-stripped), and we already hold the 100 articles locally at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/onyx.jsonl ({id, prompt, article}). Many reports are Chinese (DRB prompts are bilingual), so length/density stats need language normalization before comparison to the human reference profile.
input.corrected: Claim stands as written. Two precisions: (a) the README's "54.92 / rank 1" is a submission-time snapshot under the old Gemini-2.5-Pro RACE judge — the current Gemini CSV puts onyx at 54.54 (TrajectoryKit 54.92), and onyx is absent from the current GPT-5.5-judge leaderboard entirely, so it has no standing under the live evaluator; (b) for structure measurement use eval_results/onyx_cleaned_results.jsonl (or the local drb_corpus/onyx.jsonl), not report_logs/*.md, which prepend question + research-task blocks. Sources: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onyx-dot-app/onyx_deep_research_bench/main/README.md ; GitHub API repo/contents listings ; /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/leaderboard_gemini25pro_judge.csv and leaderboard_gpt55_judge.csv.
